title: 07375_Outlier_Gradient_Analysis_Efficiently_Identifying_Detrimental_Training_Samples_for_Deep_Learning_Models
positive_examples: Enabling influence estimation for deep models allows practitioners to assess whether training samples are beneficial or detrimental to performance.
Can make models more interpretable and performant.
Paves the way for significant positive societal impact, especially with the increased adoption of larger and deeper neural networks such as LLMs.
negative_examples: Outlier analysis algorithms have a fundamental limitation of how to specify the budget for outlier detection, which is a non-trivial hyperparameter optimization problem.
Outlier gradient analysis might not be as useful if the data is very high quality and there are no outlying gradient samples.
There are limitations to the proposed approaches that can be overcome in future work.
